_doc: Documented LEGITIMATE surface-pair overlaps and phantom-table exceptions.
Any surface-pair overlap NOT in `overlaps` below is treated as a new
duplicate-creation candidate and FAILs the gate.
Adding an entry here is the explicit 'this is intentional' switch.
Each entry MUST cite the reason -- usually 'role-based views over
shared canonical sources' (admin / seller / worker accessing the
same data model from different angles).

overlaps.reason: Role-views over marketplace canonical sources. marketplace-admin moderates listings + resolves disputes (platform staff role); marketplace-seller manages their own listings + reads disputes against them (seller role). Both legitimately read marketplace_listings / marketplace_orders / marketplace_disputes / hive_audit_log -- the data model is the same, the access predicate differs.
Role-views over marketplace canonical sources. marketplace-admin = platform moderation; marketplace.html = buyer-facing browse + checkout. Different audiences, same data backbone.
Role-views over marketplace canonical sources. marketplace-seller = seller managing own listings; marketplace.html = buyer browse + checkout. Different audiences, same backbone.

Role-views over PM/asset canonical sources. logbook = worker field-recording entry point (also wraps OCR / voice / visual defect intake + inventory transactions); pm-scheduler = supervisor cadence planning (uniquely owns pm_scope_items). Both legitimately touch pm_assets + pm_completions + logbook + asset_nodes. Different role + each surface owns a distinct unique table (logbook: inventory_items/transactions, equipment_reading_templates, fault_knowledge; pm-scheduler: pm_scope_items).

near_duplicate_columns.reason: Boolean flag + timestamp pair -- canonical Postgres convention, not drift. kyb_verified is the gating boolean; kyb_verified_at records when verification happened.
Same boolean + timestamp pattern as kyb_verified / kyb_verified_at. Legit.
